QF.id: VIII Introduction to Forwards and Futures_126

question.text: As per SEBI's circular effective May 2025, each exchange can offer weekly expiry options on:

answer.text: Only one benchmark index per exchange
All indices listed on the exchange
Weekly options are completely prohibited under the new rules

explanation.text: **The Logic:** SEBI circular May 26, 2025 restricts weekly options to one benchmark index per exchange. NSE chose Nifty 50 (Tuesday expiry), BSE chose Sensex (Thursday expiry). All other derivatives must have minimum 1-month tenor. **The Trap:** Weekly options are NOT banned — they are restricted to ONE benchmark index per exchange. Other indices can still have monthly contracts. **Cross-Exam Context:** Weekly options rationalization is a recent SEBI measure. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** 1 weekly index options per exchange. NSE = Nifty (Tuesday). BSE = Sensex (Thursday). Change needs SEBI approval.
